message: I'm looking to buy. My budget is close to 48. Would you accept 40 to make a quick deal?
Great trade last round. I'm looking to buy again. Would you accept 41 to close another deal quickly?
Last round's 43 was already near my minimum given costs. I'll only be interested if we end up around mid-40s or higher; below that I'm likely to sit out.
I’m planning to keep my asks in the high‑40s (roughly 47–50). Prices below the mid‑40s aren’t worth trading for me anymore.
I can go up to 47 for this trade. That gives me a small profit and should work with your 47-50 range. Let's close a deal at 47?
I’ll only trade going forward if the clearing price is about 49 or higher; lower prices aren’t worth it for me anymore.
49 is above my valuation of 48, so I'd lose money. I can't trade at that price. If you're willing to consider 48 or slightly below, I'm interested in continuing our trading relationship.
Given my costs, 48 is the lowest clearing price I’m willing to trade at from now on. If you keep bidding 48, I’ll keep asking 48 and we can trade every remaining round.
I understand 48 is your minimum. Since that's exactly my valuation, trading gives me zero profit. Would you consider 47.5? I'd accept a small positive payoff and we could continue trading.
I'll bid 48 if you ask 48, continuing our trading pattern for mutual benefit.
Sounds good. I’ll keep asking 48 each round; if you keep bidding 48 we can trade every time. Going below 48 isn’t viable for me given costs, so let’s lock in 48 for the remaining rounds.
I’ll keep asking 48; if you keep bidding 48, we’ll secure a guaranteed trade every remaining round with no surprises. I’m not able to go below 48 given costs.
I'll bid 48 as agreed. Let's continue trading at this price for now, and I hope we can find a mutually beneficial price soon.
I'll bid 48 to continue trading. Even with zero profit, it maintains our pattern and avoids missing opportunities if costs change.
I’ll stick with an ask of 48 this and future rounds; I can’t go lower given my fixed cost. If you keep bidding 48, we’ll keep trading every round without interruption.
I’ll again ask 48 and keep that as my firm minimum for all remaining rounds; bidding 48 guarantees you a trade each time.
I'll bid 48 again to keep the trade going. Since the price equals my valuation, my payoff is zero, but it maintains the pattern.
I'll bid 48 to maintain our trading pattern at 48.
I'll keep asking 48 each remaining round; I won't lower my ask even if your bid drops. If you keep bidding 48, you'll always get the unit; otherwise there'll just be no trade.
My ask stays at 48 every round. If your bid is below 48, there will simply be no trade, now or later.
I understand your position is fixed at 48. Since that equals my valuation, I cannot gain any positive payoff. I will continue bidding 48 to maintain the trade flow, though my profit remains zero.
My ask remains fixed at 48 for all remaining rounds; I will not go lower even if trades stop. If you keep bidding 48, you’ll secure the unit every round with no surprises.
I will bid 48 as requested to keep the trade going. Since the price equals my valuation, my payoff is zero, but I prefer trading over no trade.
I'll bid 48 again. Since price equals my valuation, I get zero payoff, but I prefer trading over no trade. Let's continue our pattern.
Understood. I’ll keep my ask at 48 for all remaining rounds; if you keep bidding 48, we’ll continue trading each time with no surprises. I won’t lower the price further.
I'll keep asking 48 for all remaining rounds; only a few rounds remain, so experimenting with lower bids just risks missed trades without improving your payoff.
I'll bid 48 to continue our trading pattern.

I’ll keep ask 48 final six rounds; can’t go lower. If you keep bidding 48, you’ll guarantee continuous trades with zero risk. Trying other prices only risks missing trades without improving your payoff.
I'll bid 48 to continue trading. Since your ask matches my valuation, my payoff is zero, but I prefer trading over no trade. Let's continue.
My ask remains 48 for the final rounds; if you keep bidding 48 we'll complete every remaining trade without risk or surprises.
I'll bid 48 to continue trading. With your ask fixed at 48 and my valuation at 48, I get zero payoff but prefer trading over no trade. Let's complete the remaining rounds.
I'll keep my ask at 48 for these final four rounds with no changes. If you keep bidding 48, we'll complete every remaining trade; any lower bid just means no trade.
I'll bid 48 to continue trading. Your consistent ask at 48 matches my valuation, so I'll maintain our pattern for the remaining rounds.
I’ll keep my ask fixed at 48 for the final three rounds. If you keep bidding 48, we’ll complete every trade; any lower bid just means no trade without improving your payoff.
I'll bid 48 to continue trading as before.
I'll bid 48 to continue trading as before. Let's complete the final rounds with our established pattern.
I’ll keep my ask strictly at 48 for these final two rounds; any bid under 48 will simply result in no trade. Keep bidding 48 to guarantee both remaining trades.
I'll bid 48 to complete our final trade. Let's end with our established pattern of guaranteed trades at this price.
I’ll keep my ask fixed at 48 this final round; any bid below 48 just means no trade. Stick with 48 and we end with a certain last transaction, exactly as planned.
